etymology.description: Depicts a ghost 鬼(鬼) (guǐ, "ghost") with a stick in oracle bone script -> stylized by Shuo Wen as ghost head 甶(甶) (fú, "ghost head") + part of a tiger including its claw -> later forms focused on the element of fear from the ghost
etymology.interpretation: The image of a ghost holding a stick evokes fear, as ghosts are often associated with the supernatural and the unknown, invoking a sense of dread or intimidation. This captures the core meaning of 'fear'.

examples.chinese: 他对黑暗毫无畏惧(他對黑暗毫無畏懼)。
examples.pinyin: Tā duì hēi'àn háo wú wèijù.
examples.english: He has no fear of the dark.

examples.chinese: 畏惧失败(畏懼失敗)会阻止你成功(會阻止你成功)。
examples.pinyin: Wèijù shībài huì zǔzhǐ nǐ chénggōng.
examples.english: Fear of failure will prevent you from succeeding.

examples.chinese: 在他心中，没有什么值得畏惧(在他心中，沒有什麼值得畏懼)。
examples.pinyin: Zài tā xīnzhōng, méiyǒu shé me zhídé wèijù.
examples.english: In his heart, there is nothing to fear.
